factors.summary: Demand is expected to accelerate with a 4% growth forecast for North American industry equipment rental revenue.

evidence.text: In 2017, we expect that the majority of our end markets will continue to experience solid demand for equipment rental services. Specifically, we expect that North American industry equipment rental revenue will increase approximately 4 percent, with similar growth, on a constant currency basis, expected in both the U.S. and Canada.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand with a 4% growth forecast, which is a positive sign for revenue.

factors.summary: Pricing power is declining as rental rates decreased by 2.2% year-over-year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining pricing power due to a 2.2% year-over-year decrease in rental rates suggests a headwind.

factors.summary: Volume trends are growing with a 3.1% increase in the volume of OEC on rent.

evidence.text: The volume of OEC on rent increased 3.1 percent year-over-year;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: A 3.1% increase in the volume of OEC on rent indicates growing demand, which is a positive trend.

factors.summary: End market momentum is strong, particularly in construction and industrial sectors.

evidence.text: We expect that the majority of our end markets will continue to experience solid demand for equipment rental services. Specifically, we expect that North American industry equipment rental revenue will increase approximately 4 percent;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong end market momentum, particularly in construction and industrial sectors, supports a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions are delivering measurable savings through Lean initiatives.

evidence.text: We achieved the anticipated run rate savings from the Lean initiatives in 2016 and expect to continue to generate savings from these initiatives;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Measurable savings from Lean initiatives indicate effective cost management, which is positive for margins.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through Lean management techniques.

evidence.text: A continued focus on 'Lean' management techniques, including kaizen processes focused on continuous improvement;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving productivity through Lean management techniques suggests efficiency gains, which is positive.

factors.summary: Management provides forward guidance expecting 4% growth in North American rental revenue.

evidence.text: In 2017, we expect that the majority of our end markets will continue to experience solid demand for equipment rental services. Specifically, we expect that North American industry equipment rental revenue will increase approximately 4 percent;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's forward guidance of 4% growth in North American rental revenue indicates confidence in future performance.

factors.summary: Management signals confidence through expansion and investment initiatives.

evidence.text: We plan to open at least 17 specialty rental branches/tool hubs in 2017 and continue to invest in specialty rental fleet to further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expansion and investment initiatives signal strong management confidence in the company's future prospects.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on specialty rental expansion and cross-selling.

evidence.text: We plan to open at least 17 specialty rental branches/tool hubs in 2017 and continue to invest in specialty rental fleet to further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear strategic focus on expansion and cross-selling indicates positive outlook.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include macroeconomic indicators affecting demand.

evidence.text: In 2017, based on our analyses of industry forecasts and macroeconomic indicators, we expect that the majority of our end markets will continue to experience solid demand for equipment rental services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Solid demand expectations across end markets despite macroeconomic sensitivities.

factors.summary: Catalysts include Project XL initiatives and specialty rental branch openings.

evidence.text: The implementation of Project XL, which is a set of eight specific work streams focused on driving profitable growth through revenue opportunities and generating incremental profitability through cost savings across our business; and We plan to open at least 17 specialty rental branches/tool hubs in 2017 and continue to invest in specialty rental fleet to further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Project XL and specialty branch openings are strong growth drivers.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are favorable with United Rentals maintaining advantages in scale and customer service.

evidence.text: Although the equipment rental industry is highly fragmented and diverse, we believe that we are well positioned to take advantage of this environment because, as a larger company, we have more extensive resources and certain compelling competitive advantages. These include a fleet of rental equipment with a total original equipment cost ('OEC'), based on the initial consideration paid, of $9.0 billion, and a national branch network that operates in 49 U.S. states and every Canadian province, and serves 99 of the 100 largest metropolitan areas in the United States.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Scale and customer service advantages provide strong competitive position.

factors.summary: Differentiation is maintained through scale, customer service, and extensive product offerings.

evidence.text: These include a fleet of rental equipment with a total original equipment cost ('OEC'), based on the initial consideration paid, of $9.0 billion, and a national branch network that operates in 49 U.S. states and every Canadian province, and serves 99 of the 100 largest metropolitan areas in the United States. In addition, our size gives us greater purchasing power, the ability to provide customers with a broader range of equipment and services, the ability to provide customers with equipment that is more consistently well-maintained and therefore more productive and reliable, and the ability to enhance the earning potential of our assets by transferring equipment among branches to satisfy customer needs.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Differentiation through scale, service, and product range is a significant strength.

factors.summary: Capital expenditure outlook is positive with investments in specialty rental branches and fleet expansion.

evidence.text: We plan to open at least 17 specialty rental branches/tool hubs in 2017 and continue to invest in specialty rental fleet to further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investments in specialty branches and fleet expansion support growth.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving with actions such as debt redemption and new issuances.

evidence.text: In 2016 and 2015, we took a number of positive actions related to our capital structure that have significantly improved our financial flexibility and liquidity, including: Redeemed all of our 5 3/4 percent Senior Secured Notes, 8 3/8 percent Senior Subordinated Notes, 8 1/4 percent Senior Notes and 7 3/8 percent Senior Notes; Issued $1 billion principal amount of 4 5/8 percent Senior Secured Notes due 2023; Issued $800 principal amount of 5 1/2 percent Senior Notes due 2025; Issued $750 principal amount of 5 7/8 percent Senior Notes due 2026; Issued $750 principal amount of 5 1/2 percent Senior Notes due 2027; Amended and extended our ABL facility, and increased the size of the facility to $2.5 billion; and Amended and extended our accounts receivable securitization facility, and increased the size of the facility to $625.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved capital structure with lower interest debt indicates positive leverage.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is strong with $1.18 billion in available liquidity.

evidence.text: As of December 31, 2016, we had available liquidity of $1.18 billion, including cash of $312.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong liquidity position with $1.18 billion available supports growth initiatives.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is creating value through expansion and fleet enhancement.

evidence.text: In 2016 and 2015, we took a number of positive actions related to our capital structure that have significantly improved our financial flexibility and liquidity, including: Redeemed all of our 5 3/4 percent Senior Secured Notes, 8 3/8 percent Senior Subordinated Notes, 8 1/4 percent Senior Notes and 7 3/8 percent Senior Notes; Issued $1 billion principal amount of 4 5/8 percent Senior Secured Notes due 2023; Issued $800 principal amount of 5 1/2 percent Senior Notes due 2025; Issued $750 principal amount of 5 7/8 percent Senior Notes due 2026; Issued $750 principal amount of 5 1/2 percent Senior Notes due 2027; Amended and extended our ABL facility, and increased the size of the facility to $2.5 billion; and Amended and extended our accounts receivable securitization facility, and increased the size of the facility to $625.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows significant positive actions improving financial flexibility and liquidity.
